assessment_items.question_content:
There are $12$ soup cans placed in $3$ equal rows.

![12 cans in 3 equal rows](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d20f11a72ec87741a71f0a010e1a51c90b05854) 

**Which expression will tell us how many soup cans are in each row?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\greenD{12}$ cans in $\blueD3$ equal rows.

![Same image with the rows numbered 1 through 3 and horizontal lines between the rows](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/89294a792d2e1721cffdb87a93c5fcc46b2a5941)

How can we find out how many cans are in each row?

We can find how many cans are in each row by dividing:

$\greenD{\text{total}}\div\blueD{\text{ number of rows}}$

So, $\greenD{12}\div\blueD{3}$

The following expression will tell us how many soups cans are in each row:

- $\greenD{12}\div\blueD3$

We can find how many buses are in each row by dividing:

So, $\greenD{24}\div\blueD{6}$

The following expression will tell us how many buses are in each row:

- $\greenD{24}\div\blueD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $8$ cups in a row. There are $32$ total cups.

![32 cups with 8 cups per row](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4ce3efe7644c2b5fe3270ede70821dacc3afd9ff) 

**Which expression will tell us how many rows of cups there are?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\greenD{32}$ cups with $\purpleC8$ cups in each row.

![Same image with the cups in a row numbered 1 through 8 and horizontal lines between the rows](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cf7657147cde03232968914f1ddd655eeb3d2c1)

How can we find out how many rows of cups there are?

We can find how many rows of cups there are by dividing:

$\greenD{\text{total}}\div\purpleC{\text{ size of the rows}}$

So, $\greenD{32}\div\purpleC{8}$

The following expression will tell us how many rows of cups there are:

- $\greenD{32}\div\purpleC8$

$\greenD{\text{total}}\div\purpleC{\text{ size of the rows}}$

The following expression will tell us how many groups of people there are:

- $\greenD{20}\div\purpleC2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which image could represent $20 \div 4?$**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can relate division to arrays:

$\begin{align}
\greenD{\text{total}}\div\blueD{\text{ number of rows}}=\purpleC{\text{ number of objects in each row}}\\\\
\greenD{\text{total}}\div\purpleC{\text{ number of objects in each row}}=\blueD{\text{ number of rows}}
\end{align}$
$\greenD{20} \div \purpleC4$ equals $\greenD{20}$ total dots split into rows of $\purpleC4$.
This array has $\greenD{20}$ total dots split into rows of $\purpleC4$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

This image represents $20 \div 4$:  

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![An array of twenty green dots arranged in 5 rows of 4 dots each.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f43e589cfb9e9fa0c5bfd3b671047a552e5f9c5d)
![An array of sixteen blue dots arranged in 4 rows of 4 dots each.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1fa361b0d128fcc1d986c6ba5f3303dc9855fdc)
![An array of twenty five red dots arranged in 5 rows of 5 dots each.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce2939c1352e7415cbcf53be9b00ae89be8f5980)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions could represent the array shown below?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD3$ rows with $\purpleC6$ dots in each row.


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\greenD{18}$ total dots.

The following expressions represent the array:

- $\greenD{18}\div\purpleC6$
- $\greenD{18}\div\blueD3$

options.alt: An array of orange dots arranged in 3 rows of 6 dots each.
